text: The surgeon performs an EDL biopsy on a suspicious nodule in the left upper lobe, followed by the appearance of significant bleeding within the chest cavity. The lung is retracted anteriorly, revealing a large aortic hematoma with active bleeding from the descending thoracic aorta.

The surgeon applies compression to control bleeding from the descending thoracic aorta, followed by conversion to an anterior thoracotomy. Post-operative imaging reveals a large hematoma in the chest cavity.
